title: amaryllis × decora
intro: Amaryllis × decora, also known as the Belladonna Lily or Bellflower, is a hybrid plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, derived from the Amaryllis belladonna and Hippeastrum species. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments due to its alkaloid content, including atropine, scopolamine, and hyoscyamine. The health benefits of Amaryllis × decora include its use as an antispasmodic, anticholinergic, and sedative, helping to alleviate symptoms of insomnia, anxiety, and muscle spasms. Amaryllis × decora is traditionally prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or dried extract for medicinal purposes, although its use is often cautioned against due to its potential toxicity and side effects.
